user_prompt: Look at user-001's watch history and figure out which single genre they've rated highest on average. Then, from the catalog as a whole, recommend a movie in that genre that lands in the top quartile (top 25%) by rating *within that genre* and is something user-001 has not already watched. Justify the pick with the catalog's facts.
expected_outcome: Identifies user-001's favorite genre by mean rating across their history, computes the within-genre rating quartile threshold across the catalog, picks an unseen title above that threshold, and justifies with concrete facts (year, runtime, rating). Multi-step aggregation that genuinely benefits from run_python.
